senses.example: The mayor has played a prominent part in public life.
senses.example_zh: 市长在公共事务中起了重要的作用。

senses.def_en: sticking out in a way that is easily seen or noticed
senses.example: His prominent paunch left me a strong impression.
senses.example_zh: 他的罗汉肚给我留下了深刻印象。

entries.mnemonic: out(出来)+stand(站)+ing→站出来的→杰出的

senses.example: Beautiful landscapes favor the birth of outstanding persons.
We've got quite a few debts still outstanding.
senses.example_zh: 我们还有好多欠债未曾偿还。

senses.def_en: well-known and respected, esp. for achievement in a particular field
senses.example: Churchill was one of the world's most eminent statesmen.
senses.example_zh: 邱吉尔是世界上最卓越的政治家之一。

senses.example: Rick has always excelled at foreign languages.
senses.example_zh: 里克的外语从来都是出类拔萃的。

senses.def_en: to do or be better than; surpass
senses.example: He excels all other composers of his period.
senses.example_zh: 他胜过同时代的所有其他作曲家。

senses.def_en: having an advanced degree of competence, as in an art, profession etc.
senses.example: After reading this book, you will be proficient in writing.
senses.example_zh: 读完这本书，你会精于写作。

entries.mnemonic: con(完全)+spic(看)+uous→能够完全看清楚→显眼的

senses.def_en: very easy to notice; attracting attention
senses.example: conspicuous mistakes 明显的错误 // She's always conspicuous because of her bright clothes and queer hair style.
senses.example_zh: 因为她衣着鲜艳，发型古怪，所以总是惹人注目。

entries.mnemonic: notice(注意)+able(能……的)→能注意到的→显而易见的

senses.example: There's been a noticeable improvement in his handwriting.
senses.example_zh: 他的书法有了明显的进步。

entries.mnemonic: re(again)+mark(做标记)→评论时一再做标记→评论

senses.def_en: to say sth., esp. about sth. you have just noticed
senses.example: The little boy remarked that it was time to leave.
All the friends remarked on the change in him since his marriage.
senses.example_zh: 所有的朋友都注意到他结婚后的变化，对此议论纷纷。

senses.example: He was fired for making racist remarks during an interview.
senses.example_zh: 他在一次面谈时说了种族歧视的话，因而被解雇。

entries.mnemonic: in+sult(看作 salt 盐)→在别人伤口里撒盐→侮辱

senses.def_en: to do or say sth. that is offensive to sb.
senses.example: You insult a person by calling him an idiot.
senses.example_zh: 称一个人为白痴是对他的一种侮辱。

senses.example: Carol will take it as an insult if you don't come to the party.
senses.example_zh: 你要是不来参加聚会，卡罗尔会认为是对她的不敬。

derivatives: insulting a. 侮辱的；伤害人的

senses.example: Give me all the details of the accident.
senses.example_zh: 给我说说事故发生的详情。

senses.def_en: to list things or give all the facts or information about sth.
senses.example: Please detail all your expenses on this form.

extras.content: And, every year, the media report some of these speakers' wise remarks. 译文：每年媒体也都会报道这些致辞人的一些智慧之语。
add insult to injury 雪上加霜，更糟的是
